title: FBI arrests custody company CEO‘s son over alleged $46M crypto theft
summary: FBI director Kash Patel posted a photo of a handcuffed John Daghita, as well as one of seized items including cash, thumb drives, a phone and devices resembling hardware wallets.

title: Ripple quietly appears inside Wall Street’s stock-clearing system as it expands XRP payments platform
summary: Ripple is sharpening its argument that it can help institutions move value across traditional rails, stablecoins, and blockchain networks. On March 2, DTCC’s National Securities Clearing Corporation updated its MPID directory to add Ripple-owned “Hidden Road Partners CIV US LLC” for its first trade.

title: SEC proposes ‘token taxonomy’ for interpreting crypto under securities laws
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission has proposed an interpretive framework for applying federal securities laws to digital assets that would carry more weight than staff-level guidance.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Cardano can now be used to pay at 137 Spar stores across Switzerland
summary: Supermarket chain Spar now accepts ADA payments at 137 stores in Switzerland as crypto payment options expand in the country.

title: Revolut makes second attempt at US bank charter, names new CEO for US business
summary: Revolut’s renewed push for a US banking license follows a failed 2021 attempt, as the $75 billion fintech expands globally.

title: NYSE owner ICE invests in OKX at $25B as tokenized stocks plan takes shape
summary: Intercontinental Exchange will take a board seat at OKX and plans to bring NYSE-listed tokenized stocks and derivatives to the crypto exchange’s users in 2026.

title: Bitcoin bears ‘annihilated’ as analysis sees $65K support test next
summary: Liquidity analysis suggests bulls are in control after Bitcoin’s move to $74,000, though a support retest could take BTC price action nearly $10,000 lower.

title: Crypto platform aims to let retail investors buy IPO shares at the same price as Wall Street insiders
summary: Most people only see IPOs after the price resets at the open. Institutional investors and select clients receive allocations at the offering price, while everyone else waits for the exchange to start trading and buys at the market price. title: UAE central bank says financial system stable amid missile and drone attacks
summary: More than 1,800 crypto businesses operate in the UAE, with over 600 Web3 companies based in Dubai’s DMCC free zone.

title: Coinbase execs face new lawsuit seeking damages, insider profit clawbacks
summary: The lawsuit seeks damages for Coinbase, governance reforms and the return of compensation and profits allegedly earned by insiders amid the company’s compliance failures.

title: Reform UK tops donations with millions from Thailand-based crypto investor: Report
summary: Nigel Farage’s Reform UK reportedly secured a second boost of 3 million British pounds ($4 million) from Thailand-based crypto investor Christopher Harborne

summary: Bitcoin slid to $63,030 after US-Israel strikes on Iran triggered a risk-off cascade across markets. From there, BTC rallied to $74,000 intraday on Mar. 4, a roughly 17% rebound. summary: Kraken has cleared a regulatory hurdle that crypto firms have chased for years: direct access to the Federal Reserve’s core payments infrastructure. summary: Bitcoin rose above $70,000 today for the first time since early February, extending a rebound that is starting to look less like a brief relief rally and more like a market trying to reverse momentum after months of heavy selling. summary: Tether has landed a Big Four accounting firm’s name on a reserve report tied to its US strategy. title: $1 Billion Floods Back Into Crypto Funds, Snapping Five-Week $4B Bleed
summary: CoinShares reported $1 billion weekly turnaround, driven by Bitcoin buying and renewed investor appetite across major markets.
